# Visual Studio 18.5 lands with AI debugging at a price, devs still feeling blue

[Tim Anderson Tim Anderson](https://www.devclass.com/author/tim-anderson)

Published fri 17 Apr 2026 // 13:24 UTC

https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.devclass.com%2Fai-ml%2F2026%2F04%2F17%2Fvisual-studio-185-lands-with-ai-debugging-at-a-price-devs-still-feeling-blue%2F5218068 https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.devclass.com%2Fai-ml%2F2026%2F04%2F17%2Fvisual-studio-185-lands-with-ai-debugging-at-a-price-devs-still-feeling-blue%2F5218068 https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.devclass.com%2Fai-ml%2F2026%2F04%2F17%2Fvisual-studio-185-lands-with-ai-debugging-at-a-price-devs-still-feeling-blue%2F5218068 https://bsky.app/intent/compose?text=Visual%20Studio%2018.5%20lands%20with%20AI%20debugging%20at%20a%20price%2C%20devs%20still%20feeling%20blue%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.devclass.com%2Fai-ml%2F2026%2F04%2F17%2Fvisual-studio-185-lands-with-ai-debugging-at-a-price-devs-still-feeling-blue%2F5218068 https://www.reddit.com/submit?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.devclass.com%2Fai-ml%2F2026%2F04%2F17%2Fvisual-studio-185-lands-with-ai-debugging-at-a-price-devs-still-feeling-blue%2F5218068&title=Visual%20Studio%2018.5%20lands%20with%20AI%20debugging%20at%20a%20price%2C%20devs%20still%20feeling%20blue https://api.whatsapp.com/send?text=Visual%20Studio%2018.5%20lands%20with%20AI%20debugging%20at%20a%20price%2C%20devs%20still%20feeling%20blue%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.devclass.com%2Fai-ml%2F2026%2F04%2F17%2Fvisual-studio-185-lands-with-ai-debugging-at-a-price-devs-still-feeling-blue%2F5218068

Visual Studio 2026 18.5 arrives with two headline changes – a smarter code suggestion system and an AI-powered debugger. Yet developer frustration over color contrast and forced updates continue to overshadow the improvements.

Many developers are familiar with the problem: they are typing in the code editor and multiple assistants intervene with suggested code, causing distraction and confusion. Even within the Microsoft stable there are several of them.

DEVCLASS AD

IntelliSense is the older system, based on static analysis without generative AI and providing code completion and help text. AI-driven IntelliCode ranks suggestions and provides context-aware and whole line completions. GitHub Copilot suggests and generates whole blocks of code and is subscription-based but with a free version for limited use.

Until this release, IntelliSense and Copilot suggestions could [appear simultaneously](https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/Avoid-IntelliSense-and-Copilot-conflicts/11038262), confusing users about what keys like Tab will do and causing cognitive overload.

A solution is to disable Copilot, but for those using multiple code completion assistants, the new Visual Studio release "prioritizes the IntelliSense list and shows only one suggestion at a time."

DEVCLASS AD

This small victory for a pre-AI world should not be misread as a retreat from Copilot. 18.5 also features a new debugger agent workflow which, Microsoft [said](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/stop-hunting-bugs-meet-the-new-visual-studio-debugger-agent/), "represents a fundamental shift in how we think about IDEs."

The new workflow, called agentic bug resolution, starts with an issue link or chat prompt describing the bug. Copilot will inspect the application, generate failure hypotheses, and set conditional breakpoints in the code. Next, the agent runs the application in debug mode, inspects the failure, and, all going well, proposes a fix.

While this sounds like a time-saver, we note that it also converts a standard developer task into something that now costs AI tokens each time it is used, meaning it is only a benefit if the AI fix is quicker to find and equally or more effective than what the human developer would have done without it.

More AI debugging is not a top developer request. Rather, devs still want to [bring back the blue theme](https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/Once-more-we-ask-for-the-Blue-Theme-bac/11027127) from Visual Studio 2022. This request is not just based on liking a certain color scheme; it reflects complaints of eye strain and a worse overall experience.

In Visual Studio 2026, the number of color tokens was [reduced](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/extensibility/migration/modernize-theme-colors?view=visualstudio) by around 87 percent as part of a migration to a semantic color system.

"The effect is straightforward: Token reduction → Loss of granular control → Reduced contrast → Lower readability → Reduced productivity. For developers who spend entire working days inside the IDE, this is not an aesthetic complaint. It directly affects cognitive load and output quality," [said](https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/Once-more-we-ask-for-the-Blue-Theme-bac/11027127#T-N11048595) one developer.

Microsoft closed an issue on this as "out of scope" but has left a similar issue open with a promise to review the complaints and propose "potential next steps."

DEVCLASS AD

![Auto-update in Visual Studio is unpopular with some, but tricky to disable](https://image.devclass.com/5218070.webp?imageId=5218070&x=0.00&y=0.00&cropw=100.00&croph=100.00&width=960&height=288&format=jpg)

Auto-update in Visual Studio is unpopular with some, but tricky to disable

Another common gripe concerns forced Visual Studio updates, which now seem to afflict this developer tool as well as Windows itself. By default, a dialog appears periodically with the options "Update now" or "postpone," where the default is to update when Visual Studio closes, but a user [reports](https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/VS2026---updates-are-now-defaulting-to/10989823#T-N11052177) that "after several postpones, Visual Studio finally decided to update by itself."

Microsoft [said](https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/VS2026---updates-are-now-defaulting-to/10989823#T-N10998041): "The primary reason for this feature is to help keep your environment secure and ensure you have the latest features, performance improvements, and reliability fixes without delay the next time you open Visual Studio."

Many developers consider that they should decide when to update. "I've had this interrupt meetings, instructional lectures, and important sessions where I am running the console in debug mode," [said](https://developercommunity.visualstudio.com/t/VS2026---updates-are-now-defaulting-to/10989823#T-N11063375) one.

A workaround has been found, and is given in the issue thread referenced above, involving an edit to a configuration file called state.json, with options not available through the settings user interface.
